newsSince: Material positive developments in the window: Vertiv completed its acquisition of Italian heat-exchange specialist ThermoKey S.p.A. on June 12 (after the prior Strategic Thermal Labs deal), expanding its EMEA thermal/heat-rejection portfolio for AI data centers. It also held a 2026 Investor Conference laying out long-term growth through 2030, reinforced the FY26 sales guidance raised earlier in Q2, and drew fresh analyst PT raises (RBC $435 outperform, Mizuho $380) against a ~$15B backlog and ongoing NVIDIA cooling/power co-development. Shares +12% since June 9.

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: ThermoKey close (Jun 12), 2030 investor-day roadmap and PT raises are genuinely positive fundamentals, but on this crowding-sensitive list a $125B name +85% YTD that ran +12% since Jun 9 is re-crowding, not de-crowding. Strong fundamentals only modestly help residual asymmetry. Small +.
verdicts.bull: ThermoKey + Strategic Thermal Labs deepen thermal stack; ~$15B backlog, raised FY26 guide, 2030 roadmap, NVIDIA co-development.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck thesis fully intact, but it is consensus and crowded after +85% YTD at a $125B cap.
verdicts.bear: On a residual-alpha screen the easy money is gone; +12% in two weeks adds momentum money, hurting asymmetry.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 print confirming raised guidance and backlog growth from AI-factory liquid-cooling demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind / AI-capex digestion; multiple compression on a heavily-owned name.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list (power/cooling/electrical): clear fundamental progress since Jun 9 — ThermoKey acquisition closed Jun 12, 2026 investor day with 2030 roadmap, FY26 guidance reinforced, PT raises, ~$15B backlog. On a fundamentals-first lens this strengthens execution; move up vs peers.
verdicts.bull: Liquid-cooling/power leader for AI factories; ThermoKey+STL expand thermal chain, ~$15B backlog, raised guide, 2030 growth roadmap, NVIDIA partnership.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck exposure scarce and strengthening, but valuation embeds heavy AI-capex assumptions.
verdicts.bear: Execution and pricing must keep delivering into a $125B cap; AI-capex pause would hit hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results confirming raised FY26 guidance; thermal-portfolio integration and backlog conversion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI data-center capex slowdown; competitive pricing pressure in cooling/power.
